user.title: BMS IR — add a device
user.description: Name the device and enter the IP address of the Broadlink universal remote (e.g. 192.168.1.50).

method.title: How should we find the code?
method.description: {manufacturer} has {count} codes. Choose how to identify the right one.

autodetect.title: Automatic scan (power sensor)
autodetect.description:
Choose the power sensor (smart plug / power meter) the AC is plugged into. The script sends each of the {count} codes in turn and detects which one makes the power rise.

IMPORTANT: turn the AC OFF with its own remote before starting. The scan may take a few minutes — please wait.

model.title: Choose model / code
model.description: Pick a code to test for {manufacturer}. Codes available: {remaining}. The code file downloads automatically. If several match, test them one by one and keep the one that works.

test.title: Test: {model} (#{code})
test.description:
{status}

A test signal was sent to the AC. Did it react (turn on / beep / change mode)? Choose the result.

finish.title: Save and place in a room
finish.description: Working code: {model} (#{code}). Choose a room (area) for the device and optionally link sensors.

error.cannot_connect: Could not connect to that IP. Make sure the Broadlink is powered, on the same network, and the IP is correct.
error.download_failed: Could not download the code file. Check internet access or pick another code.
error.scan_no_match: The scan finished but no code turned the AC on. Try another manufacturer or pick manually.

abort.no_more_codes: No more codes left to test for this manufacturer. Try a different manufacturer.

init.title: BMS IR — sensors
init.description: Change the linked sensors for this device.
